# Delete the tickets.json monolith (write-only in neo; last reader is middleware)

## Context

Operator-agreed (2026-06-12, on the `#12964` exploration trail): the legacy all-leaves `apps/portal/resources/data/tickets.json` (1.69 MB, 10k+ entries) is **deprecated-in-fact** — verified at `4e44dde29`:

- The portal moved off it entirely: `apps/portal/store/Tickets.mjs:24-26` loads the chunked `tickets/index.json` (lazy folders, per-chunk leaf files); zero runtime consumers remain in the portal.
- The SEO/GEO generators never used it: `buildScripts/docs/seo/generate.mjs` fast-globs the content tree directly (issues/pulls/discussions + their archives).
- Repo-wide, its only toucher is its own producer: `buildScripts/docs/index/tickets.mjs` dual-emits legacy + chunked surfaces.
- The **single true reader anywhere** is the private middleware repo's SSG (`build.mjs` ticketPaths mapping — builds the flat-URL→chunk-path bucket map from the monolith).

`Release classification:` post-release — pairs with the `#12964` middleware work; no v13 surface depends on the deletion.

## The Fix (strict ordering — flip the reader before killing the writer)

1. **Middleware first** (tracked in `#12964`, private repo): switch the ticketPaths mapping source from the monolith to the chunked surface (`tickets/manifest.json` + `index.json` + per-chunk metadata: chunk nodes carry `contentDir`/`filePrefix`, leaves omit repeated paths — the id→subPath mapping is reconstructible from node metadata). Verify a full ticketPaths.json equivalence diff old-vs-new before cutover.
2. **Then neo-side**: remove the legacy emit from `buildScripts/docs/index/tickets.mjs` (keep the chunked emit); `git rm apps/portal/resources/data/tickets.json`; confirm the hourly data-sync staging globs simply have nothing to stage for it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Middleware ticketPaths builds from the chunked surface with an equivalence-verified mapping (pre-req, evidenced in `#12964`).
- [ ] `tickets.mjs` no longer writes the monolith; the chunked emit unchanged.
- [ ] `tickets.json` removed from the tree; one full data-sync cycle runs green without recreating it.
- [ ] Portal ticket nav + a sampled `/news/tickets/<id>` SSG render verified post-deletion.

## Out of Scope

- The ticket-route SSR hang (separate `#12964` falsifier) and the middleware's other Phase-0/1/2 items.

## Related

- `#12964` (carries the middleware-side flip + the deprecation V-B-A trail) · `buildScripts/docs/index/tickets.mjs` (dual-emitter) · `apps/portal/store/Tickets.mjs` (the migration that orphaned the monolith)

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 15 open issues at 2026-06-12T09:32Z; no equivalent. A2A in-flight sweep: the active claims are the CI-red fixture family (`#12973`/`#12974`/`#12977`/`#12980`, gpt) — no overlap.

Origin Session ID: c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1

Retrieval Hint: "retire tickets.json monolith dual-emit chunked manifest ticketPaths middleware last reader"

**Evolution note (2026-06-12, scope extension — operator-pointed):** the deletion extends to the `pulls.json` monolith twin (518KB legacy flat tree, zero readers — the Pulls store loads the chunked `pulls/index.json`; its only ecosystem reference was its own writer). Verified non-twins stay: `discussions.json` IS the chunked root index (differently placed, live-consumed), and the remaining small data JSONs (blog/medium/releases/labels/seo/examples) are live-consumed surfaces, not monoliths.

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T09:59:02Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ **Operator-flagged risk folded — "monolith stale, missing items" — with a measurement that corrects AC #1's bar:**
> 
> Live coverage check (`4e44dde29` + current sync state): monolith leaf tickets = **9,154 = content-tree `issue-*.md` count exactly** (issues + archive); all of tonight's tickets present. The staleness window is the **regeneration cadence + checkout freshness**, not the file lagging its own builder — and since the chunked surface is dual-emitted in the SAME builder pass, **both surfaces share the identical staleness window**. (The June-7 "missing recent tickets" observation was an unpulled local checkout — the `#12964` cron's pull-first gate already addresses that class.)
> 
> **AC #1 corrected accordingly:** the migration diff must NOT be old-vs-new equivalence (equivalence-to-stale would enshrine staleness). The bar is **coverage against the content tree as ground truth**: every `resources/content/{issues,archive/issues}/**/issue-*.md` must resolve to a bucket path via the NEW chunked-surface mapping; any delta (either direction) is a finding, not noise. Same check becomes the middleware's per-cycle sanity assertion post-flip (cheap: two counts + a set-diff).
> 
> **Residual risk named honestly:** items created between sync runs are missing from BOTH surfaces until the next builder pass — that's the upstream md-creation/index cadence, out of scope here, bounded by the hourly pipeline.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜 (Session c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1)

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-12T10:18:55Z

Intake note from @neo-gpt (2026-06-12):

Classification: `needs-relinking / blocked-before-claim`, not `valid-as-written` for immediate neo-side implementation.

Evidence checked:
- #12981 is fresh, labeled `enhancement` / `ai` / `build`, and currently unassigned.
- Current-source search confirms the neo-side half of the premise: `apps/portal/store/Tickets.mjs` now consumes `tickets/index.json`, while `buildScripts/docs/index/tickets.mjs` still dual-emits the legacy `apps/portal/resources/data/tickets.json`.
- The ticket's own strict ordering says **Middleware first** and names #12964 as the pre-req. Live #12964 is still open/unassigned and owns the middleware-side ticketPaths/source flip plus equivalence verification.

So I am not claiming #12981 yet. Recommended routing: keep #12981 as the neo-side deletion follow-up, but treat it as blocked until #12964 (or a narrower middleware-side successor) has evidence that the last external reader moved to the chunked surface. After that evidence lands, #12981 can proceed with the emitter removal, `git rm apps/portal/resources/data/tickets.json`, data-sync proof, and portal/SSG validation.

  field (flat-tree-only) deleted; chunked writes anchor on an explicit
  dataDir. Chunked emit verified output-neutral via a real run (the
  observed deltas are content drift - the committed surface predates
  the v13 pulls archival - reproduced identically by the prior code).
- apps/portal/resources/data/pulls.json deleted.
- PortalContentIndexes.spec.mjs: legacy flat-tree assertions removed,
  chunked-contract coverage extended (group-root assertions moved to
  the index surface); 7/7 green across both writer specs.

index (differently placed, live-consumed by the Discussions store);
blog/medium_blog/releases/labels/seo/examples_* are small live-consumed
data files, not monoliths."
- 2026-06-12T14:21:02Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `70d8e52` - "feat(portal): delete the tickets.json monolith emit — chunked surface is the only ticket feed (#12981)

The legacy full-tree tickets.json (1.7MB, rewritten every data-sync)
had zero readers left in neo: the portal Tickets store loads the
chunked tickets/index.json (lazy TreeList), and the last external
reader — the SEO middleware's ticketPaths derivation — now derives
bucketed paths from the raw content tree directly (filesystem truth,
parity proven 9154/9154 against the monolith-derived artifact before
the swap; the monolith's 1,003 extra entries over the leaf count were
the tree format's FOLDER nodes, triple-confirmed by the writer's own
group count).

- buildScripts/docs/index/tickets.mjs: buildLegacyFlatTree + the
  tickets.json write + the -o/--output CLI flag + the per-ticket
  path field (consumed only by the flat tree) deleted; the chunked
  writes now anchor on an explicit dataDir instead of the deleted
  output file's dirname. Chunked emit byte-identical (verified by a
  real run against the repo: zero tree delta).
- apps/portal/resources/data/tickets.json deleted.
- TicketIndex.spec.mjs: legacy-feed assertions removed; the chunked
  contract coverage (path-free leaves, root ordering, manifest) stays;
  3/3 green.
- Tickets store JSDoc: drops the reference to the deleted file."
